title: Connectors
description: Database connector configuration: a named map of connectors, each discriminated by `type`. Supported engines: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB.

connectors.description: Connectors keyed by a unique connector name.

propertyNames.description: Connector name: letters, digits, underscore, hyphen.

connectionString.description: PostgreSQL connection URI (postgres:// or postgresql://). Mutually exclusive with `details`.
MySQL connection URI (mysql://; mariadb:// is cross-accepted — wire-compatible). Mutually exclusive with `details`.

type.description: Discriminator: MariaDB. Wire-compatible with MySQL but kept distinct for flavour-specific behavior.

connectionString.description: MariaDB connection URI (mariadb://; mysql:// is cross-accepted — wire-compatible). Mutually exclusive with `details`.

host.description: Server hostname or IP. When `ssh` is set, this is the database address as reachable from the SSH host.

password.description: Login password. Omit for trust/peer/IAM auth.

host.description: Server hostname or IP. When `ssh` is set, this is the database address as reachable from the SSH host.

database.description: Database name. MySQL has no implicit default database.

host.description: Server hostname or IP. When `ssh` is set, this is the database address as reachable from the SSH host.

database.description: Database name. MariaDB has no implicit default database.

secret.description: A secret value. Either a literal string, or an object naming where to read it from. A bare string is shorthand for { "from": "literal", "value": <string> }.

oneOf.description: Shorthand for a literal secret value.

from.description: Read the secret from an environment variable.

env.description: Name of the environment variable holding the secret.

from.description: Read the secret from a file.

path.description: Path to a file whose (trimmed) contents are the secret.

sslmode.description: PostgreSQL-style SSL mode. disable: no TLS. allow: plaintext, TLS only if forced by server. prefer (default): TLS if the server offers it, silent plaintext fallback otherwise. require: TLS but no certificate verification. verify-ca: verify the server certificate chain against the CA. verify-full: verify the chain AND the hostname. For MySQL/MariaDB, any value other than disable enables TLS; verify-ca/verify-full enforce certificate validation.

tls.description: TLS / SSL settings. Certificate and key paths are PEM files on the machine running the connector.

rootCertFilePath.description: Path to the CA root certificate (PEM) used to verify the server. libpq sslrootcert / Node `ca`. Typically required for verify-ca/verify-full against a private CA.

clientCertFilePath.description: Path to the client certificate (PEM) for mutual TLS. libpq sslcert / Node `cert`.

clientKeyFilePath.description: Path to the client private key (PEM) for mutual TLS. libpq sslkey / Node `key`.

ssh.description: Optional SSH tunnel through a bastion/jump host. The connector opens a local port forward through this host to `details.host:details.port`.

username.description: SSH user. Defaults to the current OS user ($USER) when omitted.

sshKeyFilePath.description: Path to the SSH private key (OpenSSH/PEM).

passphrase.description: Passphrase for the SSH private key, if it is encrypted. Leave unset for an unencrypted key.
